# Contract: Yes Reader FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `005-yes-cc-parser` | **Date**: 2026-07-15
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + ICICI + HDFC + SBI `engine-ffi` contracts. The "external interface" is the
exported UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the consumer-facing form.
**Reuses** the existing `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` custom types, the `Direction` enum, and the
`ParsedStatement`/`ParsedTransaction` records **unchanged** — Yes adds **two functions and no new
type**.

---

## Exported functions

### `read_yes_statement`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn read_yes_statement(lines: Vec<String>, full_text: String) -> ParsedStatement` |
| Swift | `public func readYesStatement(lines: [String], fullText: String) -> ParsedStatement` |

**Contract**
- Parses **already-extracted** text `lines` (one statement row per element) + `full_text` (used only
  for enrichment). **Never** opens a file or PDF (FR-015).
- **Single layout**: `DD/MM/YYYY <details … Ref No> <Merchant Category> <amount> Dr|Cr`. Produces one
  `ParsedTransaction` per matching row; non-matching lines (headers/summaries/balances/totals) are
  ignored (FR-005); rows matching the shape but failing date/amount parse go to
  `ParsedStatement.errored_lines` (FR-014) — **never throws, never aborts**. A statement with no
  matching rows returns empty `lines` with no error.
- **Direction from the terminal `Dr`/`Cr` marker**: `Cr → .credit`, `Dr → .debit` (via
  `classify(desc, dir, None)`) — never the amount's sign or the description's wording; the marker wins
  even when the description contains a credit/debit word (e.g. `PAYMENT RECEIVED`, `REFUND`,
  `CASHBACK`) (FR-008/009, SC-004).
- **Amount** is an exact non-negative `Decimal` with scale preserved; Indian thousands grouping is
  stripped (FR-006/007, SC-005). Money never crosses as `f64`.
- Enriches `period_start` + `period_end` (both parsed from the `<date> To <date>` match) and
  `card_last4` (masked PAN via the `Card Number` anchor) from `full_text` when present; absent → left
  `nil`. `3561XXXXXXXX6686` → `cardLast4 == "6686"`; a mask exposing **fewer than four** trailing
  digits → `nil` (never fabricated) (FR-010/011/012).
- **Reconciliation carve-out (FR-013, SC-013)**: the result carries **only** rows + period + last-4
  (+ errored lines). It exposes **no** printed debit/credit totals, even when `full_text` contains
  `Purchases … Dr` / `Payment & Credits Received … Cr` summary lines — those are ignored.
- **Pure & deterministic**: identical input ⇒ identical output; no network, clock, locale, or global
  state (FR-016/019). Total function — no failure surfaced to Swift.

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**
- Call with PDFKit-extracted lines + full text. Treat `errored_lines` as review items; still consume
  every `lines` entry. Do not infer direction from `amount` — use `direction`. Treat `cardLast4 ==
  nil` as "unknown", never as a value to fabricate. Do not expect printed totals (they are not in the
  model this slice).

### `yes_claims`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn yes_claims(full_text: String) -> bool` |
| Swift | `public func yesClaims(fullText: String) -> Bool` |

**Contract**
- Returns `true` iff `full_text` contains the Yes Bank issuer marker (`"YES BANK"`, case-insensitive)
  — the document-plausibility gate (FR-001).
- MUST return `false` for other issuers' statements (e.g. an ICICI, HDFC, or SBI document) so a
  statement is never misattributed to Yes (FR-002, US1-AC4). Pure, deterministic, no I/O.

> Internally: `read_yes_statement` wraps `read_lines(&YesReader, …)` (single layout — **not** the HDFC
> composite); `yes_claims` delegates to `claims(&YesReader, …, "YES")`. `lib.rs` re-exports both
> functions (as it does the ICICI/HDFC/SBI pairs); `statement/mod.rs` gains `pub mod yes;`.

---

## Types (all reused — no new type)

- `record ParsedStatement` / `record ParsedTransaction` — **unchanged** from the ICICI/HDFC/SBI slices.
  Yes **populates `periodStart`** (like HDFC/SBI) and `cardLast4 == "6686"`. **No `printed_total_*`
  fields exist** (reconciliation carve-out).
- `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` → Swift `.debit` / `.credit` (P1, `uniffi::Enum`).
- Custom types `Decimal`↔`String`↔`Foundation.Decimal` and `NaiveDate`↔ISO-8601 `String`
  (P1, in `ffi.rs` + `uniffi.toml`) — **unchanged**. Money never crosses as `f64`.

> Swift shape (for reference, unchanged): `ParsedStatement { bankCode: String; lines:
> [ParsedTransaction]; erroredLines: [String]; periodStart: String?; periodEnd: String?;
> cardLast4: String?; confidence: Double }`.

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-022/024)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + `yes.rs` unit tests):
- **Golden parity**: `read_yes_statement(basic.lines, basic.full_text)` equals the fixture's
  `expected` exactly — rows
  `2026-04-29 / 9000.00 / Credit / INR / "PAYMENT RECEIVED BBPS - Ref No: RT0001"` and
  `2026-04-19 / 100.00 / Debit / INR / "UPI_EXAMPLE STORE IND - Ref No: RT0002 Miscellaneous Stores"`;
  `period_start 2026-04-17`; `period_end 2026-05-16`; **`card_last4 "6686"`**; `errored_lines` empty
  (SC-001/003/009).
- **Determinism**: two calls on the same input are equal.
- **Wrong issuer**: `yes_claims("ICICI Bank …") == false`, `yes_claims("GSTIN of SBI Card") == false`;
  `yes_claims(basic.full_text) == true` (FR-002, SC-002).
- **Direction from marker**: a row whose description contains credit words (`PAYMENT RECEIVED` /
  `REFUND` / `CASHBACK`) but whose terminal marker is `Dr` classifies **Debit** (marker beats
  wording); `Cr` → Credit (SC-004).
- **Reconciliation carve-out**: a `full_text` that also contains printed `Purchases … Dr` /
  `Payment & Credits Received … Cr` lines yields **only** rows + period + last-4 (SC-013).
- **Malformed row**: a line matching the Yes shape but with an unparseable date lands in
  `errored_lines`, valid rows still returned, no panic (reuses the existing seam behaviour).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`)** — "core ↔ Swift Yes parse":
- `readYesStatement(...)` over the two golden lines → 2 `lines`; `lines[0]` = `2026-04-29`,
  `Decimal(string:"9000.00")`, `.credit`, `"INR"`, `"PAYMENT RECEIVED BBPS - Ref No: RT0001"`;
  `lines[1]` = `2026-04-19`, `Decimal(string:"100.00")`, `.debit`,
  `"UPI_EXAMPLE STORE IND - Ref No: RT0002 Miscellaneous Stores"`.
- `periodStart == "2026-04-17"`; `periodEnd == "2026-05-16"`; **`cardLast4 == "6686"`**.
- `yesClaims(fullText:) == true` for the Yes text and `false` for an ICICI/SBI string.
- Amounts compared as `Foundation.Decimal` value-equality (exact; never float).

---

## Stability / compatibility
- Adding two functions is additive; reusing the records means **no wire-format change** and no new
  Swift type.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts,
  rebuilt by `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**, not
  the generated files, is the reviewed source of truth.
